# Introduction

Cache is a crucial part of any high-performance application. It stores frequently accessed data, reducing database or API calls and improving response times.

## Why Use Cache?
- **Speed**: Serve data instantly from memory or fast storage.
- **Scalability**: Reduce load on databases and external services.
- **Cost**: Lower infrastructure costs by reducing repeated expensive operations.

## Cache Manager
Warlock provides a unified, developer-friendly API to interact with cache. The [`CacheManager`](./cache-manager) lets you use any cache driver with a consistent set of methods.

## Available Cache Drivers
Warlock ships with several drivers out of the box:

1. [Redis Cache Driver](./redis) — Best for distributed, production-grade caching.
2. [Database Cache Driver](./database) — Store cache in your database for persistence and querying.
3. [File Cache Driver](./file) — Simple, persistent, file-based cache.
4. [Memory Cache Driver](./memory) — Fast, in-memory cache (resets on restart).
5. [Memory Extended Cache Driver](./memory-extended) — Like memory, but resets TTL on every access (sliding expiration).
6. [LRU Memory Cache Driver](./lru-memory) — In-memory cache with Least Recently Used eviction.
7. [Null Cache Driver](./null) — Disables caching (useful for testing).

## Quick Guide: Which Driver Should I Use?
| Use Case | Recommended Driver |
|----------|-------------------|
| Production, multi-server, distributed | Redis |
| Need database persistence with querying | Database |
| Local development, simple persistence | File |
| Fastest, ephemeral, single-process | Memory |
| Need sliding expiration | Memory Extended |
| Limited memory, want LRU eviction | LRU Memory |
| Testing, disable cache | Null |

## Advanced Features
- **Namespaces** for grouping cache keys
- **Global Prefixes** to avoid key collisions
- **Custom Drivers**: [Create your own](./make-your-own-cache-driver)
- **Driver Switching**: Change drivers at runtime
- **Multi-driver strategies** (coming soon)
- **Built-in Logging**: All cache operations are automatically logged
- **TTL Management**: Automatic expiration handling

Ready to get started? Check out [Cache Configurations](./configurations) or jump to a driver above!
